title: Decoder producer/ranker r64 k1 physical wrapper
hypothesis: After the r64/k1 ready-valid RTL equivalence gate passed, a bounded macro-style physical wrapper tying logit_rank_r64_l16_k1 to candidate_stream_merge_fifo_k1_l16_t16_d16 should provide the first measured PPA anchor for the producer-to-ranker interface before scaling to r128, wider windows, or tighter producer coupling.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What is the first bounded physical PPA/timing anchor for the r64/k1 producer-to-ranker ready-valid wrapper after equivalence has passed?
include: ready-valid wrapper with the same stream contract as the equivalence harness
exclude: new shared SRAM or NoC arbitration

expected_benefit: turns the passed stream-equivalence result into the first measured PPA anchor for the connected ranker/merge interface
keeps the initial physical target at the smallest r64/k1 integration point selected by the memory-integration plan
separates bounded wrapper PPA from later producer, memory, and NoC coupling experiments
risks: top-level macro pin pressure may still affect floorplan feasibility even though the wrapper models a connected ready-valid stream
the wrapper does not include the full output-projection GEMM producer
the first sweep uses a conservative single floorplan point and may need follow-up perimeter/core-size tuning

required_evaluations.objective: Materialize and physically probe the r64/k1 ready-valid producer-to-ranker wrapper after equivalence passed, recording bounded PPA/timing before scaling producer-ranker integration.

expected_result.reason: If the wrapper is physically measured, use it as the first producer-ranker PPA anchor before r128 or producer-coupled scaling. If blocked, adjust wrapper/floorplan before relying on physical integration cost.
